metadata.purpose: Social movement ecosystem map for Benjamin Von Wong — activist artist research
metadata.note: Budget/size figures are approximate from available public records and reporting through early 2025

major_orgs.name: The Nature Conservancy (TNC)
major_orgs.tactics: Land acquisition and conservation easements; science-based habitat protection; working with corporations on supply chain; influencing government policy; 125M+ acres protected globally
key_wins: Protected more land than any org in history

major_orgs.name: Greenpeace International
major_orgs.tactics: Direct action, ship-based interventions, media-friendly spectacle, corporate campaigns, blockades, banner drops — zero corporate or government funding accepted
key_wins: Forced Shell to abandon Arctic drilling 2015
Amazon zero deforestation commitments from major food companies

major_orgs.name: World Wildlife Fund (WWF)
major_orgs.tactics: Species protection, land/sea conservation, corporate partnerships, policy advocacy, certification schemes (FSC, MSC), government lobbying
key_wins: CITES appendix listings protecting endangered species

major_orgs.name: Sierra Club
major_orgs.tactics: Legal action, electoral campaigning, litigation, grassroots organizing, Beyond Coal campaign (closed 350+ coal plants in USA), lobbying
key_wins: Beyond Coal: retired 350+ US coal plants

major_orgs.name: 350.org
major_orgs.tactics: Divestment campaigns, mass mobilization, day of action coordination globally, pressure on financial sector, youth-led organizing, climate justice framing
key_wins: Divestment commitments from 1,500+ institutions ($40T+ in assets)

major_orgs.name: Environmental Defense Fund (EDF)
major_orgs.tactics: Science + policy + economics hybrid approach; corporate partnerships to shift practices; methane monitoring satellite (MethaneSAT); market-based environmental solutions

major_orgs.name: Sunrise Movement
major_orgs.tactics: Youth-led direct action, electoral organizing, sit-ins (Nancy Pelosi's office 2018), primary challenges, Green New Deal popularization
key_wins: Put Green New Deal on Democratic Party agenda
Mobilized youth climate vote in 2020 election
Sit-in catalyzed AOC-Pelosi Green New Deal meeting

major_orgs.name: ClientEarth
major_orgs.tactics: Strategic climate litigation; suing governments for climate plan failures; sued Shell board of directors directly (2023); legal capacity-building globally
key_wins: Multiple EU government climate plan legal victories
Shell board lawsuit (landmark, ultimately unsuccessful but precedent-setting)

top_foundations.name: Bezos Earth Fund
top_foundations.focus: Climate, nature, equity — $10B total commitment through 2030

top_foundations.name: Bloomberg Philanthropies
top_foundations.focus: Climate — Beyond Carbon, Beyond Coal, clean energy transition

top_foundations.name: ClimateWorks Foundation
top_foundations.focus: Climate philanthropy intermediary — redistributes to frontline orgs

top_foundations.name: Rockefeller Brothers Fund
top_foundations.focus: Fossil fuel divestment, climate policy, democratic resilience

government_funders: US EPA, DOE, USAID Environmental programs

genuine_allies: Patagonia (1% for the Planet, entire company transferred to environmental trust 2022)
IKEA Foundation (climate, children, refugees)
Microsoft ($1B Climate Innovation Fund)
Salesforce ($100M commitment to reforestation)
conflicts_of_interest: Shell/BP funding WWF and EDF while expanding fossil fuels
BlackRock 'ESG' commitments while being largest investor in deforestation companies
Amazon Bezos Earth Fund while Amazon's own emissions grew massively

mega_donors: Jeff Bezos ($10B Bezos Earth Fund)
Laurene Powell Jobs (Emerson Collective — climate + education)
Jeremy Grantham (Grantham Foundation)
Tom Steyer (NextGen Climate, Galvanize Climate Solutions)
Hansjörg Wyss (Wyss Foundation — 30x30 land protection)
Mike Bloomberg (Bloomberg Philanthropies)
Yvon Chouinard (Patagonia — transferred company to environmental trust)

celebrities.name: Leonardo DiCaprio
celebrities.specific_impact: LDF Foundation granted $100M+ to environmental causes; Before the Flood documentary drove massive awareness; co-produced multiple environmental documentaries; supported Sea Shepherd, Amazon Watch, indigenous land rights

celebrities.name: Greta Thunberg
celebrities.specific_impact: Single-handedly catalyzed global school strikes movement; addressed UN, Davos, COP summits; nominated for Nobel Peace Prize multiple times; moved climate up political agenda in 2018-2020

celebrities.name: Jane Fonda
celebrities.specific_impact: Fire Drill Fridays civil disobedience campaign in DC 2019-2020; arrested multiple times; mobilized Hollywood community; produced documentary 'What Can I Do?'

celebrities.name: David Attenborough
celebrities.specific_impact: Blue Planet II drove UK plastic bag ban and global plastics policy debate; Our Planet Netflix series; addressed UN climate summits; COP26 people's advocate role

celebrities.name: Mark Ruffalo
celebrities.specific_impact: Co-founded Water Defense; Solution Project advocacy for 100% renewable; frequent testifier before Congress; anti-fracking activism with real policy impact in NY State

celebrities.name: Yvonne Aki-Sawyerr
celebrities.specific_impact: Transform Freetown initiative planted 1M trees; flood prevention through urban forests; model for climate-vulnerable cities globally

government_champions.name: Christiana Figueres
government_champions.what_they_did: Architected the Paris Agreement 2015; 'Mission 2020' initiative; co-founded Global Optimism; transformed impossible into inevitable narrative

government_champions.name: John Kerry
government_champions.what_they_did: US-China bilateral climate deal enabling Paris Agreement; Inflation Reduction Act advocacy; global bilateral climate diplomacy

government_champions.name: Patricia Espinosa
government_champions.what_they_did: Shepherded Paris Agreement implementation rulebook (Katowice, Madrid, Glasgow)

government_champions.name: Jacinda Ardern
government_champions.what_they_did: Zero Carbon Act 2019; climate emergency declaration; banned new offshore oil and gas exploration

government_champions.name: Al Gore
government_champions.what_they_did: An Inconvenient Truth (2006) — mainstream climate awareness; Nobel Peace Prize 2007; Climate Reality Project trained 40,000+ climate advocates

allies.what_they_did: 1% For The Planet founding member; funded grassroots orgs; sued Trump administration over monument rollbacks; 2022 Yvon Chouinard transferred company ownership to environmental trust — generates ~$100M/yr permanently for environment
Mission Zero: 1994 commitment to zero environmental impact by 2020, largely achieved; inspired corporate sustainability movement globally; Climate Take Back
Transformed from coal/oil to world's largest offshore wind company; divested all oil and gas; model corporate transition story

villains.why: Internal documents show knew about climate change since 1970s; funded climate denial campaigns for decades; ongoing lobbying against climate legislation
Hundreds of millions into climate denial organizations; funded ALEC, Heritage Foundation, Heartland Institute; systematically blocked US climate legislation
World's largest private coal company; funded climate denial; lobbied against Paris Agreement; multiple bankruptcies while avoiding environmental cleanup costs

reformed.after: Net Zero by 2050 target; invested billions in renewables — though critics note backsliding under Looney/Murray Auchincloss on targets
Sustainable Living Plan under Polman; net zero supply chain by 2039; stopped deforestation in palm oil supply chain partially

major_orgs.name: NAACP (National Association for the Advancement of Colored People)
major_orgs.tactics: Legal challenges, voter registration drives, legislative advocacy, boycott campaigns, electoral mobilization through NAACP chapters nationwide
key_wins: NAACP Legal Defense Fund landmark cases

major_orgs.name: Movement for Black Lives (M4BL)
major_orgs.tactics: Coalition coordination across BLM chapters; policy platform development (BREATHE Act, defund/divest campaigns); cultural strategy; international solidarity
key_wins: Put police abolition/reform into mainstream political discourse 2020

major_orgs.name: Color of Change
major_orgs.tactics: Online petitions and pressure campaigns, corporate accountability, electoral organizing, DA accountability campaigns, media industry reform
key_wins: Pushed corporations to cut ties with ALEC (American Legislative Exchange Council)
Criminal justice reform through DA elections

major_orgs.name: Equal Justice Initiative (EJI)
major_orgs.tactics: Legal representation for death row inmates; historical research and documentation; National Memorial for Peace and Justice (lynching memorial); public education about racial history
key_wins: Reversed/commuted death sentences for hundreds of wrongly convicted
National Memorial for Peace and Justice opened 2018 — national conversation about lynching history
Just Mercy book/film — mainstream narrative shift

major_orgs.name: ACLU (American Civil Liberties Union)
major_orgs.tactics: Constitutional litigation, legislative advocacy, lobbying, public education, policy research
funders: Flood of small donors post-2016 ($50M raised in one weekend after Muslim ban)

major_orgs.name: Advancement Project
major_orgs.tactics: Strategic litigation, policy advocacy, on-the-ground organizing support, racial justice research
key_wins: Voting rights protection in multiple states

top_foundations.name: Ford Foundation
top_foundations.focus: Racial justice centerpiece — $100M pledge to BLM-adjacent orgs 2016, more since 2020

top_foundations.name: W.K. Kellogg Foundation
top_foundations.focus: Racial healing, children, food equity — strong racial equity lens

top_foundations.name: Open Society Foundations
top_foundations.focus: Criminal justice, police accountability, voting rights

top_foundations.name: Borealis Philanthropy
top_foundations.focus: Intermediary for racial justice movement funding

top_foundations.name: JPMorgan Chase Foundation
top_foundations.focus: Post-2020 racial equity pledge ($30B over 5 years, much to Black-owned businesses)

genuine_allies: Ben & Jerry's (public statements, donations to Movement for Black Lives — unusual for an Unilever subsidiary)
Patagonia (racial justice as part of social mission)
Airbnb (anti-discrimination policies, Project Lighthouse data)
conflicts_of_interest: Banks pledging racial equity while maintaining discriminatory lending patterns
Tech companies pledging diversity while underpaying and firing Black employees disproportionately
Corporations pledging post-George Floyd donations while lobbying against voting rights

mega_donors: George Soros / OSF ($220M+ announced 2020 for racial justice)
MacKenzie Scott ($281M+ to racial equity orgs 2020-2021 alone)
Pritzker family (various racial justice causes)
Melinda Gates (personal philanthropy with racial equity lens)

celebrities.name: Colin Kaepernick
celebrities.specific_impact: Taking a knee during anthem in 2016 sparked global racial justice conversation; settled with NFL for $60-80M; Nike partnership made 'Just Do It' racial justice statement; Know Your Rights Camp trains youth; became global symbol of movement

celebrities.name: Beyoncé
celebrities.specific_impact: Lemonade album as Black feminist cultural manifesto; Formation video placed at Black Lives Matter; Black Is King visual album; donated millions to bail funds and social justice orgs; leveraged massive platform with intentionality

celebrities.name: Bryan Stevenson
celebrities.specific_impact: Just Mercy sold millions; film adaptation; Created National Memorial for Peace and Justice; TED talk 27M+ views; mainstream conversation about mass incarceration

celebrities.name: Ava DuVernay
celebrities.specific_impact: 13th documentary on Netflix — prison-industrial complex documentary reached millions; Selma (voting rights); ARRAY distribution for underrepresented filmmakers

celebrities.name: LeBron James
celebrities.specific_impact: More Than A Vote voter registration campaign (2020); I PROMISE School in Akron ($2M/yr personal funding); refused 'shut up and dribble'; publicly spoke on police brutality; registered voters during NBA bubble

government_champions.name: John Lewis
government_champions.what_they_did: Lifetime of civil rights activism; Voting Rights Advancement Act bore his name; bridge between civil rights movement and modern racial justice; 'good trouble' philosophy

government_champions.name: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez
government_champions.what_they_did: Racial justice framing in Green New Deal; champion of defund police debates; made racial equity central to progressive Democratic coalition

government_champions.name: Cyril Ramaphosa
government_champions.what_they_did: Historical context: moved from labor/anti-apartheid to President; land reform debates

government_champions.name: Tendayi Achiume
government_champions.role: Former UN Special Rapporteur on Racism
government_champions.what_they_did: Global reparations advocacy; racial discrimination in border policies; digital racial equity reports

allies.what_they_did: Explicit public statements supporting Black Lives Matter (unusual for corporate brand); donated to Movement for Black Lives; Colin Kaepernick flavor; maintained stance under Unilever pressure
Colin Kaepernick 'Believe in Something' campaign 2018 — symbolic and financial support; racial justice ad campaigns; $140M racial equality commitments

villains.why: Platform amplified white nationalist content; slow to act on racial harassment; secret internal research showed harm to Black users
Private prison industry profits from mass incarceration which disproportionately affects Black and Brown people

reformed.before: Colluded to blacklist Kaepernick; Goodell denied racial justice framing
reformed.after: Goodell 2020 video acknowledging being wrong; $250M Inspire Change grant program over 10 years — widely criticized as inadequate but represents shift

reformed.before: Racial bias incident 2018 (two Black men arrested waiting)
reformed.after: Closed 8,000 stores for racial bias training; hired civil rights attorney; updated store policies — mixed reviews on effectiveness

major_orgs.name: UN Women
major_orgs.tactics: Norm-setting through global conventions (CEDAW, Beijing Platform); technical assistance to governments; economic empowerment programs; ending gender-based violence campaigns (HeForShe)
funders: Voluntary contributions from member states (UK, EU, USA, Nordic countries dominant)

major_orgs.name: AWID (Association for Women's Rights in Development)
major_orgs.tactics: Feminist movement building, funding advocacy, research, global forums (every 4 years), connecting feminists across South-South
key_wins: Connecting feminist funds across global South

major_orgs.name: Global Fund for Women
major_orgs.tactics: Grantmaking to grassroots women's rights orgs globally; movement building; feminist foreign policy advocacy; emergency grants
key_wins: Funded 5,000+ women's rights orgs in 175 countries
Feminist Foreign Policy adoption by multiple countries

major_orgs.name: Equality Now
major_orgs.tactics: Legal advocacy and litigation for women's rights globally; spotlight campaigns on laws that discriminate against women; FGM campaigns
key_wins: Pressure on states to repeal 'marry your rapist' laws
Legal wins on FGM in multiple countries

major_orgs.name: #MeToo / Time's Up Foundation
major_orgs.tactics: Social media mobilization, legal defense fund for survivors, industry accountability campaigns, policy change in entertainment industry
key_wins: Massive policy changes in entertainment, media, tech industries
Time's magazine 'Silence Breakers' Person of the Year
Legislative changes in California (statute of limitations)

major_orgs.name: Women's March Organization
major_orgs.tactics: Mass mobilization (largest single-day protest in US history 2017), voter registration, electoral organizing, intersectional feminist platform
key_wins: 4-5M people marched globally Jan 21 2017 (largest single-day protest in US history)
Spurred 'Year of the Woman' 2018 election results

top_foundations.name: Open Society Foundations
top_foundations.focus: Women's rights, reproductive rights, economic justice

top_foundations.name: Swedish SIDA
top_foundations.focus: Feminist foreign policy — largest government gender funder

genuine_allies: Estée Lauder Companies Foundation (domestic violence awareness — Breast Cancer Campaign)
Salesforce (equal pay audits and corrections)
Patagonia (paid parental leave, onsite childcare model)
conflicts_of_interest: Fashion brands funding women's empowerment while paying women garment workers poverty wages
Tech companies diversity pledges while maintaining massive gender pay gaps
Media companies funding Time's Up while maintaining same power structures

mega_donors: MacKenzie Scott (gave $4.2B in 2021 to social causes including major feminist orgs)
Melinda Gates (Gates Foundation + personal Pivotal Ventures)
Pritzker family
Sheryl Sandberg (Lean In Foundation, domestic violence after husband's death)

celebrities.name: Emma Watson
celebrities.specific_impact: HeForShe campaign speech at UN 2014 went viral; mobilized 1.8M male allies to sign commitment; engaged men in gender equality conversation in unprecedented way

celebrities.name: Tarana Burke
celebrities.specific_impact: Founded Me Too 2006 for survivors; when Alyssa Milano amplified in 2017, Burke's decade of work hit critical mass; guided movement's survivor-centered approach; Congressional testimony

celebrities.name: Malala Yousafzai
celebrities.specific_impact: Malala Fund raised $100M+ for girls' education; Nobel Prize youngest ever; challenged Taliban gender apartheid on world stage; personally drove policy changes in Afghanistan girls education narrative

celebrities.name: Oprah Winfrey
celebrities.specific_impact: Golden Globes speech catalyzed Time's Up movement; decades of platform for women's issues; Oprah's Angel Network grants; survivor stories on national television moved policy conversations

celebrities.name: Gloria Steinem
celebrities.specific_impact: Decades of movement leadership; Women's March organizing role; bridged second and third wave feminisms; global feminist foreign policy advocacy

government_champions.name: Justin Trudeau
government_champions.what_they_did: Feminist international assistance policy 2017; cabinet parity; equal pay legislation; national childcare program

government_champions.name: Jacinda Ardern
government_champions.what_they_did: First leader to give birth in office while serving; wellbeing budget framework; feminist leadership model globally

government_champions.name: Michelle Bachelet
government_champions.role: Former UN Human Rights High Commissioner
government_champions.what_they_did: UN Women founding head 2010-2013; First female president of Chile; abortion rights in Chile

government_champions.name: Phumzile Mlambo-Ngcuka
government_champions.what_they_did: HeForShe campaign launch; Generation Equality Forum

allies.what_they_did: CEO Marc Benioff spent $3M+ correcting gender pay gaps after internal audit revealed disparities; ongoing pay equity audits; model for corporate gender equity
Force of Nature campaign funding women in outdoor spaces; CEO is woman; genuinely equitable internal policies

villains.why: 80% of garment workers are women; poverty wages; harassment and abuse documented in factories; inadequate maternity protection
Systemic pay gap; Weinstein enabling; slow change despite #MeToo pressure

reformed.before: Multiple sexual harassment and boys' club culture scandals; CEO Mark Parker resigned
reformed.after: New CEO John Donahoe; diversity commitments; pay equity reporting; though progress contested

major_orgs.name: ILGA World (International Lesbian, Gay, Bisexual, Trans and Intersex Association)
major_orgs.tactics: UN advocacy and lobbying, global legal monitoring (State-Sponsored Homophobia report), coalition building, UN ECOSOC status
key_wins: Regular UN Human Rights Council resolutions
State-Sponsored Homophobia report shapes global policy
UNESCO and UN system LGBTQ+ inclusion advocacy

major_orgs.name: Human Rights Campaign (HRC)
major_orgs.tactics: Federal and state legislative advocacy, electoral campaigns, Corporate Equality Index (CEI), public education campaigns
key_wins: Don't Ask Don't Tell repeal advocacy

major_orgs.name: Lambda Legal
major_orgs.tactics: Strategic LGBTQ+ constitutional litigation; amicus briefs; impact litigation; HIV/AIDS rights cases
key_wins: Contributed to Obergefell v. Hodges marriage equality

major_orgs.name: OutRight Action International
major_orgs.tactics: UN advocacy, international LGBTQ+ rights, emergency responses, connecting global South orgs with international advocacy
key_wins: UN Independent Expert on Sexual Orientation and Gender Identity (mandate), defended annually

major_orgs.name: Rainbow Railroad
major_orgs.tactics: Direct emergency evacuation of LGBTQ+ people from dangerous countries; relocation support; crisis response (Chechnya, Uganda, etc.)
key_wins: Evacuated hundreds from Chechnya, Uganda, Afghanistan after Taliban; 'Escape from Chechnya' documentary; real lives saved — 7,000+ helped since founding

major_orgs.name: GLSEN (Gay, Lesbian and Straight Education Network)
major_orgs.tactics: School safety programs, Day of Silence, research on LGBTQ+ student experiences, GSA (Gay-Straight Alliance) network support
key_wins: National School Climate Survey — definitive data source
Policy wins on inclusive curricula in multiple states

top_foundations.name: Arcus Foundation
top_foundations.focus: Primary LGBTQ+ global funder AND great ape conservation — unique intersection

government_funders: Dutch MFA (largest government LGBTQ+ funder globally)
Swedish SIDA (feminist foreign policy includes LGBTQ+)

genuine_allies: Apple (Tim Cook openly gay; LGBTQ+ supplier diversity requirements; App Store anti-discrimination)
Levi Strauss (longstanding LGBTQ+ ally since 1992 domestic partner benefits)
Salesforce (Equality Act lobbying; refused to expand in states with anti-LGBTQ+ laws)
conflicts_of_interest: Companies that display rainbow logos in Western markets while operating in anti-LGBTQ+ countries without advocacy
Tech companies funding HRC while enabling anti-LGBTQ+ content on platforms

mega_donors: Jon Stryker (Arcus Foundation founder — $500M+ to LGBTQ+ causes)
Tim Gill (Gill Foundation — LGBTQ+ electoral strategy)
Elton John (EJAF)
David Bohnett (tech entrepreneur, LGBTQ+ orgs)
Mitchell Silberberg (legal/entertainment LGBTQ+ philanthropist)

celebrities.name: Elton John
celebrities.specific_impact: EJAF raised $575M+ for HIV/AIDS programs since 1992; produced Rocketman with LGBTQ+ authenticity; lobbied heads of state personally; changed HIV/AIDS stigma narrative

celebrities.name: Ellen DeGeneres
celebrities.specific_impact: Coming out on prime-time TV 1997 despite network pressure — watershed cultural moment; Ellen DeGeneres Show normalized LGBTQ+ people over 19 years to mainstream audiences

celebrities.name: RuPaul
celebrities.specific_impact: Mainstream cultural platform for drag/gender fluidity; Emmy-winning show shifted mass culture; drag normalization before legislative backlash

celebrities.name: Pose creators (Ryan Murphy, Steven Canals, Billy Porter)
celebrities.specific_impact: Largest cast of trans actors in TV history; ballroom culture brought to mainstream; Billy Porter's advocacy and visibility; Emmy wins for trans representation

celebrities.name: Laverne Cox
celebrities.specific_impact: First openly trans woman on Time magazine cover 2014; Orange Is The New Black platform; trans visibility shifted mainstream culture; Emmy nomination history-making

government_champions.name: Ban Ki-moon
government_champions.what_they_did: Explicitly championed LGBTQ+ rights at UN — historically unprecedented for SG position; 'Free and Equal' UN campaign launch

government_champions.name: Hillary Clinton
government_champions.what_they_did: Geneva 2011 speech: 'Gay rights are human rights' — landmark moment; LGBTQ+ rights integrated into US foreign policy

government_champions.name: Edgardo Vgan (Bogotá)
government_champions.what_they_did: Colombia's comprehensive LGBTQ+ rights framework — model for Latin America

government_champions.name: Nicola Sturgeon
government_champions.what_they_did: Gender Recognition Reform Bill (Scotland); explicit LGBTQ+ ally; removed historical convictions for gay men

allies.what_they_did: CEO Tim Cook op-ed 'I'm Proud to Be Gay' 2014; LGBTQ+ supplier diversity requirements; refused to build on states with anti-LGBTQ+ laws in some cases; donated $1M to Human Rights Watch
Threatened to pull out of Indiana after RFRA anti-LGBTQ+ bill 2015; similar stance on other discriminatory bills; Equality Act Congressional testimony

villains.why: CEO Dan Cathy donations to anti-LGBTQ+ organizations (Fellowship of Christian Athletes, Paul Anderson Youth Home); though company reduced giving after 2019 controversy
Propaganda supporting anti-LGBTQ+ legislation globally; amplifying 'gay propaganda' narratives

reformed.before: Donated to anti-LGBTQ+ politician Tom Emmer 2010
reformed.after: LGBTQ+ products, Pride collections; though faced conservative boycott backlash 2023 and pulled back some merchandise

major_orgs.name: IWGIA (International Work Group for Indigenous Affairs)
major_orgs.tactics: UN advocacy, documentation of rights violations, publication of 'The Indigenous World' annual report, network building, legal monitoring
key_wins: UN Declaration on Rights of Indigenous Peoples (UNDRIP) advocacy

major_orgs.name: Cultural Survival
major_orgs.tactics: Radio in indigenous languages (70+ stations globally), advocacy, community media, FPIC (Free, Prior and Informed Consent) monitoring

major_orgs.name: Amazon Watch
major_orgs.tactics: Corporate campaigns (oil companies, banks), legal support for Amazon indigenous communities, direct action support, media/documentation
key_wins: Corporate bank divestment from destructive Amazon projects

major_orgs.name: Forest Peoples Programme
major_orgs.tactics: Legal support, FPIC, rights documentation, international advocacy, capacity building with forest communities globally
key_wins: Landmark FPIC legal victories in multiple countries

major_orgs.name: Land Rights Now
major_orgs.tactics: Global campaign coordinating indigenous rights advocacy, SRHR, women's land rights; ICC communications on land grabbing
key_wins: UN recognition of indigenous territories connection to climate

major_orgs.name: Indigenous Environmental Network (IEN)
major_orgs.tactics: Pipeline resistance, Standing Rock support infrastructure, indigenous climate advocacy, anti-fossil fuel divestment in indigenous frame
key_wins: Fossil fuel divestment from indigenous perspective

top_foundations.name: Christensen Fund
top_foundations.focus: Biocultural diversity — indigenous knowledge and land

top_foundations.name: Rainforest Foundation Norway
top_foundations.focus: Forest peoples' land rights — major Norwegian fund

government_funders: Norwegian government (NICFI forest initiative — $500M+/yr for tropical forests)

genuine_allies: Patagonia (indigenous land protection, standing rock support)
REI (public lands advocacy, indigenous partnerships)
conflicts_of_interest: Mining companies CSR funding indigenous orgs while displacing communities
Oil companies FPIC consultations that are box-checking, not genuine consent
World Bank 'safeguards' that fail indigenous communities in practice

mega_donors: Leonardo DiCaprio (Amazon Watch, indigenous rights funder through LDF)
Wyss Foundation (land protection including indigenous territories)
Norwegian government (largest indigenous/forest rights funder globally)

celebrities.name: Taika Waititi
celebrities.specific_impact: Normalized indigenous representation in Hollywood; vocal on indigenous rights globally; Oscar win for Jojo Rabbit created platform

celebrities.name: Q'orianka Kilcher
celebrities.specific_impact: New World film and ongoing indigenous rights advocacy; DAPL Standing Rock direct action; testified before Congress

celebrities.name: Neil Young
celebrities.specific_impact: Honour the Treaties tour; pulled music from Spotify over COVID; vocal opposition to tar sands; donated to indigenous rights orgs

celebrities.name: Lily Cole
celebrities.specific_impact: Who Cares Wins platform; indigenous rights and climate intersection advocacy

government_champions.name: Victoria Tauli-Corpuz
government_champions.role: Former UN Special Rapporteur on Indigenous Rights
government_champions.what_they_did: Led global indigenous rights advocacy; Philippines indigenous peoples rights law; landmark reports on climate and indigenous rights

government_champions.name: Evo Morales
government_champions.what_they_did: First indigenous president; Rights of Nature constitutional provision; UNDRIP champion at UN

government_champions.name: Deb Haaland
government_champions.role: Secretary of Interior (first Native American Cabinet Secretary)
government_champions.what_they_did: Missing and Murdered Indigenous Women and People task force; boarding school federal report; Bears Ears monument restoration

allies.what_they_did: Bears Ears National Monument advocacy; standing with Standing Rock; funded indigenous-led conservation orgs; transferred company to trust that includes indigenous advocacy

villains.why: DAPL route change from white communities to Standing Rock Sioux territory; water protector criminalization; $1B+ pipeline despite tribal opposition
Niyamgiri Hills mine in India — Dongria Kondh sacred land; illegal deforestation; numerous human rights violations
Amazon deforestation directly linked to indigenous territory violations; Yanomami genocide crisis

reformed.before: Juukan Gorge sacred site destruction in Australia 2020 — 46,000-year-old caves blown up for iron ore mining
reformed.after: CEO Andrew Harding resigned; Cultural Heritage Policy reforms; FPIC processes strengthened — though indigenous communities say insufficient

major_orgs.name: ITUC (International Trade Union Confederation)
major_orgs.tactics: Global union coordination, ILO advocacy, G7/G20 lobbying, rights violation monitoring, Global Rights Index (annual report on worst countries for workers)
key_wins: ILO Convention on Violence and Harassment (C190)

major_orgs.name: Clean Clothes Campaign
major_orgs.tactics: Brand pressure campaigns, consumer mobilization, wage theft recovery, emergency response for factory disasters, Accord (Bangladesh safety agreement)
key_wins: Bangladesh Accord on Fire and Building Safety (after Rana Plaza)
Living wage campaigns in garment sector

major_orgs.name: IndustriALL Global Union
major_orgs.tactics: Global framework agreements with multinationals, supply chain union rights, trade union organizing in Global South factories
key_wins: VW, H&M, Inditex labor standard agreements

major_orgs.name: Fight for $15
major_orgs.tactics: Fast food worker organizing, legislative campaigns for minimum wage increases, strike coordination, political mobilization of low-wage workers
key_wins: $15 minimum wage in California, New York, others
McDonald's, Amazon, Walmart wage increases — responding to pressure

major_orgs.name: Economic Policy Institute (EPI)
major_orgs.tactics: Economic research and policy advocacy for workers; State of Working America reports; wage theft data; CEO vs worker pay ratios
key_wins: Policy research that shaped minimum wage and overtime rule debates
Research that countered corporate 'job killing' minimum wage arguments

major_orgs.name: Worker Rights Consortium (WRC)
major_orgs.tactics: University licensing program monitoring, factory inspections, brand accountability in collegiate supply chains
key_wins: 180+ university licensing agreements requiring labor standards
Multiple factory abuses exposed and remediated
Severance fund payments for Bangladesh garment workers

top_foundations.name: Ford Foundation
top_foundations.focus: Labor rights, economic justice, worker organizing

top_foundations.name: Mott Foundation
top_foundations.focus: Civil society broadly, labor in global south

genuine_allies: Patagonia (fair trade garment certification, living wages in supply chain)
Costco (known for above-industry wages and benefits — model)
Gravity Payments (Dan Price raised company minimum to $70K)
conflicts_of_interest: Tech companies funding worker-friendly orgs while fighting unionization
Retail foundations funding poverty reduction while paying poverty wages
Fast fashion brands funding sustainability while paying $3/day in factories

mega_donors: George Soros (OSF worker rights programs)
Pritzker family (labor issues in hospitality)
MacKenzie Scott (gave to labor orgs including SEIU and worker centers)

celebrities.name: Sara Bareilles / Lin-Manuel Miranda (Actors/artists generally)
celebrities.specific_impact: Broadway organizing drives; SAG-AFTRA strikes 2023; artist solidarity with below-the-line workers

celebrities.name: Dolores Huerta
celebrities.specific_impact: Founded United Farm Workers with Cesar Chavez; 'Si Se Puede'; grape boycotts; lifetime of labor organizing; Presidential Medal of Freedom 2012

celebrities.name: AOC / Bernie Sanders
celebrities.specific_impact: $15 minimum wage into mainstream discourse; Amazon union support; Bernie at inauguration meme normalized economic justice language for millions

government_champions.name: Sharan Burrow
government_champions.what_they_did: Led global labor movement on climate (just transition), supply chains, ILO conventions

government_champions.name: Bernie Sanders
government_champions.what_they_did: Made income inequality, $15 minimum wage, worker rights mainstream Democratic issues; Amazon union visit; Boeing strike support

government_champions.name: Lula da Silva
government_champions.what_they_did: Founded PT (Workers' Party); led Bolsa Família; union leader turned president; reelected 2022 defeating Bolsonaro

allies.what_they_did: Consistently pays above industry wages ($25+/hr); comprehensive benefits; $7B+ in wages annually; proof that worker-friendly business model is profitable
CEO Dan Price cut own salary from $1.1M to $70K to raise all employees to $70K minimum; became global case study in wage equity

villains.why: Anti-union practices; hired Pinkerton detectives to monitor workers; warehouse injury rates 2x industry average; fired organizers; fought BN8 Staten Island union victory with all legal tools available
Largest employer in USA; food stamp usage by full-time workers; blocked unions for decades; offshored manufacturing; poverty wages despite record profits

reformed.after: After Staten Island Amazon Labor Union won first election 2022, raised minimum wages; improved some safety metrics — though union-busting continues elsewhere

reformed.before: Fought $15 minimum wage lobbying for years
reformed.after: Raised average US wage to $15/hr by 2024 — driven by Fight for $15 pressure and competitive labor market

government_champions.name: Franca Salis-Madinier
government_champions.what_they_did: EU Farm to Fork Strategy — targets for organic farming (25%), pesticide reduction (50%), biodiversity

allies.what_they_did: B Corp certification for majority of business; regenerative agriculture investments; plant-based portfolio expansion; Emmanuel Faber pushed stakeholder capitalism (until ousted by activists in 2021)
Aggressive anti-dairy advertising ('like milk but made for humans'); transparency on emissions; pushed plant-based mainstream; IPO funding for expansion

villains.why: 'Worst company in the world' per multiple rankings; largest private company in US; Amazon soy deforestation driver; opaque supply chains; price-fixing history; little accountability due to private status
Glyphosate/Roundup cancer lawsuits ($10B+ settlements); GMO seed patents restricting farmer seed saving; neonicotinoid bee harm; herbicide-resistant superweed creation

reformed.after: No-deforestation palm oil commitment; Regenerative Agriculture program; full transparency on palm oil suppliers — partially — though full deforestation-free claim disputed

cross_movement_connectors.name: Ford Foundation
cross_movement_connectors.why_important: ~$700M/yr in grants; explicitly frames all work through social justice lens; one of few funders that bridges environmental AND racial justice genuinely; 'Building Institutions and Networks' approach creates durable movement infrastructure

cross_movement_connectors.name: Open Society Foundations
cross_movement_connectors.why_important: $1.2B/yr; explicitly cross-movement; George Soros attacked by authoritarian governments BECAUSE they see OSF as cross-movement democracy infrastructure; grantees span 100+ countries; connects Global South movements to international advocacy

cross_movement_connectors.name: MacArthur Foundation
cross_movement_connectors.why_important: $700M+/yr; 100&Change competition funds big bets across domains; Chicago-based with global reach; explicitly funds 'intersection' work; supports work that bridges climate and justice

cross_movement_connectors.name: Patagonia
cross_movement_connectors.why_important: Rare corporate entity that is genuinely cross-movement; its supply chain work, environmental grants, Indigenous land advocacy, and product model are all integrated; 2022 company transfer to environmental trust makes it structurally permanent

cross_movement_connectors.name: 350.org
cross_movement_connectors.why_important: Pioneered 'climate justice' framing that connects environmental movement to racial and economic justice; divestment campaigns connect finance to frontline communities; Bill McKibben's writing bridged activism and policy globally

cross_movement_connectors.name: Naomi Klein
cross_movement_connectors.why_important: This Changes Everything (2014) connected climate to capitalism; No Logo connected labor/brands; Shock Doctrine connected disaster capitalism; one of the most read cross-movement thinkers; Leap Manifesto (Canada); personal relationship with Indigenous movements

cross_movement_connectors.name: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (AOC)
cross_movement_connectors.why_important: Green New Deal — the first policy to explicitly connect climate, jobs, and racial equity; only US politician with genuine cross-movement coalition credibility; 13M Instagram followers as organizing tool; demonstrated electoral viability of intersectional platform

cross_movement_connectors.name: Oxfam International
cross_movement_connectors.why_important: $1.3B/yr budget; operations in 90 countries; connects emergency humanitarian work to long-term structural change; Gender-responsive budgeting advocacy; GROW campaign on food systems; publishes annually on inequality (Davos timing maximizes impact)

cross_movement_connectors.name: Laila Ahmadi / Dr. Vandana Shiva (archetype — movement intellectuals)
cross_movement_connectors.why_important: Movement intellectuals who cross domains are rare and powerful; Shiva's work connects seed sovereignty, women's land rights, climate resilience, and anti-corporate power — making her a connective node across multiple movements globally

cross_movement_connectors.name: Wellcome Trust
cross_movement_connectors.why_important: £35B endowment; 2019 launched 'Our Planet, Our Health' connecting climate and health — one of first major funders to explicitly bridge these domains; funds research that underpins multiple movement claims

cross_movement_connectors.name: Christiana Figueres
cross_movement_connectors.why_important: Paris Agreement architect; Global Optimism co-founder; Outrage + Optimism podcast; connects climate science, policy, business and justice communities; one of most effective cross-sector conveners globally

cross_movement_connectors.name: Leonardo DiCaprio Foundation (LDF)
cross_movement_connectors.why_important: $100M+ in grants; unusual in that a celebrity-founded fund has genuine expertise and grantee quality; funds frontline orgs that larger foundations ignore; bridges entertainment industry attention to movement organizations

power_funders.name: Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation
power_funders.ideological_lean: Technocratic liberal — believes in technology solutions, private sector partnerships, measurable outcomes; controversial for top-down approach

power_funders.name: Open Society Foundations (George Soros)
power_funders.ideological_lean: Liberal internationalist — explicitly pro-open society, anti-authoritarian; attacked by authoritarian governments as destabilizing

power_funders.name: Ford Foundation
power_funders.ideological_lean: Social justice liberal — explicitly frames all grantmaking through equity lens; 'Build Power' approach; one of first major foundations to center racial justice

power_funders.name: Bloomberg Philanthropies (Michael Bloomberg)
power_funders.ideological_lean: Pragmatic centrist — data-driven, results-focused, city-level solutions, market mechanisms

power_funders.name: Bezos Earth Fund (Jeff Bezos)
power_funders.ideological_lean: Tech solutionist — large bets, innovation focus, carbon removal technology, nature-based solutions; controversial due to Amazon environmental record

power_funders.name: Packard Foundation (David & Lucile Packard)
power_funders.ideological_lean: Conservationist, science-based — HP family foundation with strong ocean/environment focus

power_funders.name: MacKenzie Scott
power_funders.ideological_lean: Equity-focused — gives to overlooked orgs, unrestricted funds, trusts org leadership; no strings attached model disrupting philanthropy norms

power_funders.name: Wyss Foundation (Hansjörg Wyss)
power_funders.ideological_lean: 30x30 — protect 30% of land and oceans by 2030; large-scale conservation focus

power_funders.name: Arcus Foundation (Jon Stryker)
power_funders.ideological_lean: Explicitly LGBTQ+ rights globally — unique two-focus model; Jon Stryker is openly gay heir to Stryker medical devices fortune

power_funders.name: Laudes Foundation (C&A / Cofra family)
power_funders.ideological_lean: Industry transformation — fashion insiders who want to reform the industry they come from; funds both advocacy and innovation

power_funders.name: Omidyar Network / Luminate (Pierre Omidyar)
power_funders.ideological_lean: Responsible tech and democracy — eBay founder's philanthropy; believes tech can support open societies if governed well
